name: Web Graph Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the webgraph contract (#1443) in a real browser: a semantic GraphSpec in, a deterministic layered-DAG layout invents coordinates, and an SVG renderer draws themed and accessible output — scored on the three conformance axes (semantic-fidelity, theme-application, accessibility). The contract is type-only (graphs/contract.ts); the native-first layered-DAG layout (#1444), SVG renderer (#1445), adapters, and swap registries are impl in Frontier UI (#1290), so the demo supplies its own in-demo layout + renderer to prove the contract is realizable. Scored live against the src/cases/webgraph/* cases: one mark per node and one link per edge between its declared endpoints with the layered layout placing a source above its target, node fill resolved semantically from the theme palette by kind (no spec hex) and re-themeable with edge weight resolving a stroke scale, a labelled <figure><table> adjacency fallback derived from the spec, and WAI-ARIA Graphics roles (graphics-document/object/symbol) with labels derived from node/edge semantics.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the Web Graph contract (#1443). A semantic GraphSpec in, a deterministic layered-DAG layout invents coordinates, and an SVG renderer draws themed and accessible output — scored on the three conformance axes.

In-demo layout + renderer, not the FUI impl

The contract is type-only (graphs/contract.ts); the native-first layered-DAG layout (#1444), the SVG renderer (#1445), every adapter, and the swap registries are impl and live in Frontier UI. So this demo supplies its own in-demo layout + SVG renderer — honest for a browser demo — to prove the contract is realizable. The #1352 two-seam split hands COORDINATES between layout and renderer, so the in-demo renderer joins each positioned node back to the spec by id for the semantic plane.

Scored axes

The conformance section scores each axis live against the src/cases/webgraph/* cases: semantic fidelity (one mark per node, one link per edge between its declared endpoints, source above target, deterministic), theme application (node fill resolved from the palette by kind, no spec hex, re-themeable; edge weight resolves a stroke scale), and accessibility (a labelled <figure><table> adjacency fallback derived from the spec + WAI-ARIA Graphics roles with labels derived from node/edge semantics).
